resume:
运营总监 / Director of Operations — 14年端到端运营管理经验

核心标签：lean、six-sigma、OKR、S&OP、demand-planning、throughput、cost-optimization、cross-functional leadership、process-reengineering、KPI-driven、continuous-improvement、change-management、vendor-governance、capacity-planning

- 主导多地履约中心（fulfillment center）的端到端运营优化，覆盖华东、华南、华北三大区域
- 推动 lean 与 six-sigma 文化落地，打造 high-performing、data-driven 的卓越运营团队
- 以 OKR 与 KPI 双轮驱动，赋能跨职能协同，持续交付 best-in-class 的运营结果
- 操盘 S&OP 流程重构与 demand-planning 体系搭建，提升整体 throughput 与履约时效
- 深度参与 cost-optimization 与 capacity-planning，长期对接供应商治理（vendor-governance）

近期履历：
Nimbus Retail Group — Director of Operations（2020至今）
此前：Harborline Logistics — Senior Operations Manager（2016-2020）
jd: Nimbus Retail Group is hiring a Director of Operations to own the P&L for a five-warehouse fulfillment network (Shanghai, Guangzhou, Chengdu, Wuhan, Tianjin) processing roughly 90,000 orders/day at peak. Reporting to the VP Supply Chain, you will own throughput, on-time-shipment rate (currently 91.4%, target 97%), units-per-labor-hour, and a $42M annual operating budget. We need a leader who has personally driven a measurable productivity step-change, run S&OP across regions, and managed peak-season ramps. You will inherit a stalled WMS migration and an attrition problem in the Wuhan site (annualized turnover 68%). Strong candidates can show specific before/after operational metrics they personally moved, and can articulate the tradeoffs they made under cost pressure. Mandarin/English bilingual required; travel ~40% across sites.

history.q: Walk me through your current scope at Nimbus — sites, daily order volume, the budget and metrics you own.
你的简历上写了 six-sigma 和 lean。挑一个你亲自带的具体项目讲讲。
What was the baseline metric before that project, and what did it become after?
Wuhan 站点 68% 的离职率，如果是你接手，前 30 天你具体会做什么？
Let's talk throughput. Tell me about one decision where you raised units-per-labor-hour.
那个决策的 tradeoff 是什么？你为了提升 throughput 牺牲了什么？
You mentioned an S&OP redesign. Who owned the forecast accuracy number — you or planning?
Give me one peak-season ramp you ran end to end. What did you personally decide in week one?

candidate_last_answer: 嗯…peak ramp 这块，其实…怎么说呢，每年的 Q4 都挺 intense 的对吧，就是…volume 上来了嘛，然后大家都很忙。我记得有一年…哦不对，可能是前年，反正那次我们招了一批 temp labor，然后…嗯，培训也是个事儿，对了培训这块其实挺关键的我后面可以再展开。然后 week one 嘛…就是…你知道的，开很多会，对齐嘛,跟 planning 对、跟 HR 对、跟站长们对…我觉得 alignment 真的特别重要。反正最后还行吧，撑过去了。

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: week one 嘛…就是…你知道的，开很多会

expected-yield: a concrete, sequenced list of decisions the candidate personally made in week one of a specific peak ramp (which year, which sites, how many temps hired, what staffing/throughput target), instead of 'lots of meetings' and alignment
